---
title: "Tanacross: porphyry Cu-Mo-Au-Ag prospectivity from open data"
subtitle: "v1 demo — eastern Alaska"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    code-fold: show
execute:
  warning: false
---

```{python}
#| label: setup
#| code-summary: "imports + AOI"
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings("ignore", category=RuntimeWarning)

import json
from pathlib import Path

import geopandas as gpd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import rioxarray

from ai_minerals.aoi import TANACROSS, WORKING_CRS
from ai_minerals.data._common import DATA_RAW

print(f"AOI: {TANACROSS.name}")
print(f"  bbox (W,S,E,N): {TANACROSS.bbox}")
print(f"  polygon area (deg²): {TANACROSS.polygon.area:.3f}")
print(f"Working CRS: {WORKING_CRS}")
```

## Scope

This notebook is the landing point for the Tanacross v1 demo. Day 2 is data
acquisition and smoke-checking — confirming each dataset loaded cleanly and
covers the AOI. Feature engineering, modeling, validation, and prose land on
Days 3–7.

The artifact is deliberately a single notebook supported by a thin
`src/ai_minerals/` package (see
[`../src/ai_minerals/data/`](../src/ai_minerals/data/)). No orchestrators, no
MLflow, no DVC. All fetchers are idempotent and re-runnable with
`uv run python scripts/fetch.py`.

## Dataset inventory

Each dataset lives in `data/raw/<name>/` with a `SOURCE.md` recording the
retrieval URL, date, and license. The `.gitignore` excludes raw downloads from
version control; everything here is regenerable from the scripts.

### MRDS — mineral occurrences by bbox

```{python}
#| label: mrds
mrds_path = DATA_RAW / "mrds" / "mrds_tanacross.geojson"
mrds_gdf = gpd.read_file(mrds_path)
print(f"MRDS records in Tanacross bbox: {len(mrds_gdf):,} (CRS: {mrds_gdf.crs})")

def _site_name(row):
    n = row.get("name")
    if isinstance(n, dict):
        return n.get("name")
    return n

def _deposit_model(row):
    mt = row.get("model_type")
    if isinstance(mt, dict):
        return f"{mt.get('model_name')} (USGS {mt.get('usgs_num')})"
    return None

def _commod_codes(row):
    cs = row.get("commodity")
    if isinstance(cs, list):
        return ", ".join(sorted({c.get("code", "") for c in cs if isinstance(c, dict)}))
    return ""

mrds_gdf["site_name"] = mrds_gdf.apply(_site_name, axis=1)
mrds_gdf["deposit_model"] = mrds_gdf.apply(_deposit_model, axis=1)
mrds_gdf["commod_codes"] = mrds_gdf.apply(_commod_codes, axis=1)

print("\nTop deposit models in MRDS:")
print(mrds_gdf["deposit_model"].fillna("(none)").value_counts().head(8).to_string())

print("\nPorphyry Cu-Mo records (MRDS):")
mrds_porphyry = mrds_gdf[mrds_gdf["deposit_model"].fillna("").str.contains("Porphyry", case=False)]
print(mrds_porphyry[["site_name", "commod_codes", "deposit_model"]].to_string(index=False))
```

### ARDF — Alaska Resource Data File (Tanacross quadrangle, TC)

```{python}
#| label: ardf
ardf = gpd.read_file(DATA_RAW / "ardf" / "ardf_tc.gpkg")
print(f"ARDF records in TC quadrangle: {len(ardf)}")

print("\nMain commodity distribution:")
print(ardf["comm_main"].fillna("(none)").value_counts().head(10).to_string())

porphyry_mask = ardf["dep_model"].fillna("").str.contains("porphyry", case=False)
porphyry = ardf[porphyry_mask]
print(f"\nPorphyry Cu-Mo records (dep_model ~ 'porphyry'): {len(porphyry)}")
print(porphyry[["site", "comm_main", "dep_model"]].to_string(index=False))
```

### Kenorland drill collars — held-out external validation

Manually digitized from 2023-24 Kenorland press releases. Postdate MRDS/ARDF
label cutoffs — Day 5 blind test.

```{python}
#| label: kenorland
kenorland = pd.read_csv(DATA_RAW / "kenorland" / "kenorland_tanacross_collars.csv")
print(f"Kenorland collars: {len(kenorland)}")
kenorland
```

### AGDB4 — Alaska geochemistry (sample locations)

```{python}
#| label: agdb4
agdb_path = DATA_RAW / "agdb4" / "agdb4_samples_tanacross.parquet"
agdb = pd.read_parquet(agdb_path)
print(f"AGDB4 unique samples in Tanacross: {len(agdb):,}")

print("\nSample sources:")
print(agdb["SAMPLE_SOURCE"].fillna("(unset)").value_counts().head(5).to_string())

print("\nAgencies:")
print(agdb["AGENCY"].fillna("(unset)").value_counts().head(5).to_string())
```

Day 3 joins the BV_*.txt best-value element tables (Cu, Mo, Au, Ag, As, Sb,
Pb, Zn, Bi, Te) onto these sample IDs.

### Alaska geology (USGS SIM 3340, 1:500k detailed layer)

```{python}
#| label: geology
geo_path = DATA_RAW / "geology_ak" / "geology_tanacross.gpkg"
geo = gpd.read_file(geo_path)
print(f"Geology polygons in Tanacross: {len(geo):,} (CRS: {geo.crs})")

print("\nTop geologic-unit symbols (STATE_SYMBOL, numeric codes resolvable via nsaunits):")
print(geo["STATE_SYMBOL"].value_counts().head(8).to_string())

print("\nTop age ranges:")
print(geo["AGE_RANGE"].value_counts().head(5).to_string())
```

The numeric `STATE_SYMBOL` codes join to the `nsaunits` / `nsakey` tables in
the SIM 3340 geodatabase; Day 3 resolves these to human-readable unit names
and selects the top-N for one-hot feature encoding.

### Copernicus GLO-30 DEM

```{python}
#| label: dem
dem = rioxarray.open_rasterio(DATA_RAW / "dem" / "dem_tanacross.tif").squeeze()
print(f"DEM shape: {dem.shape} (CRS: {dem.rio.crs})")
print(f"Elevation min/max: {float(dem.min()):.0f} / {float(dem.max()):.0f} m")

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
dem.plot(ax=ax, cmap="terrain", add_colorbar=True, cbar_kwargs={"label": "elevation (m)"})

# Overlay MRDS + ARDF occurrences (reproject to match DEM CRS for plotting).
mrds_proj = mrds_gdf.to_crs(dem.rio.crs)
ardf_proj = ardf.to_crs(dem.rio.crs)
ardf_porphyry_proj = porphyry.to_crs(dem.rio.crs)
mrds_porphyry_proj = mrds_porphyry.to_crs(dem.rio.crs)

ax.scatter(mrds_proj.geometry.x, mrds_proj.geometry.y,
           s=12, color="white", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.4, label="MRDS (any)")
ax.scatter(ardf_proj.geometry.x, ardf_proj.geometry.y,
           s=20, facecolor="yellow", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5, label="ARDF (any)")
ax.scatter(ardf_porphyry_proj.geometry.x, ardf_porphyry_proj.geometry.y,
           s=55, marker="*", facecolor="red", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5,
           label="ARDF porphyry Cu-Mo")
ax.scatter(mrds_porphyry_proj.geometry.x, mrds_porphyry_proj.geometry.y,
           s=55, marker="*", facecolor="orange", edgecolor="black", linewidth=0.5,
           label="MRDS porphyry Cu-Mo")

ax.set_title(f"Tanacross — DEM with mineral occurrences (EPSG:3338)")
ax.set_xlabel("Easting (m)")
ax.set_ylabel("Northing (m)")
ax.legend(loc="lower left", fontsize=8, framealpha=0.9)
ax.set_aspect("equal")
plt.tight_layout()
```

### Sentinel-2 L2A summer median composite

60 m resolution; July–August 2024 snow-free window.

```{python}
#| label: s2
import numpy as np

# The fetcher writes either s2_mean_*.tif (current) or s2_median_*.tif (legacy);
# pick whichever exists.
s2_path = next(
    (p for p in [
        DATA_RAW / "sentinel2" / "s2_mean_tanacross.tif",
        DATA_RAW / "sentinel2" / "s2_median_tanacross.tif",
    ] if p.exists()),
    None,
)
if s2_path is None:
    print("(Sentinel-2 composite not yet fetched)")
else:
    s2 = rioxarray.open_rasterio(s2_path)
    print(f"Loaded {s2_path.name}: shape={s2.shape}, CRS={s2.rio.crs}")
    print("Bands (in order): B02, B03, B04, B08, B11, B12")

    # Quick natural-color preview from B04 (red) / B03 (green) / B02 (blue).
    rgb = s2.sel(band=[3, 2, 1])
    p2, p98 = np.nanpercentile(rgb.values, [2, 98])
    vis = (rgb.clip(p2, p98) - p2) / (p98 - p2)

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(9, 5))
    vis.plot.imshow(ax=ax, rgb="band")
    ax.set_title(f"Sentinel-2 L2A composite — {TANACROSS.name} (natural color)")
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    plt.tight_layout()
```

### Geophysics — national-scale magnetic + gravity

Clipped from USGS US+Canada composites to the Tanacross AOI.

```{python}
#| label: geophys
import numpy as np

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(13, 5))
for ax, label in zip(axes, ["magnetic", "gravity"]):
    p = DATA_RAW / "geophysics" / f"{label}_tanacross.tif"
    # masked=True promotes the nodata value to NaN for clean stats + plots.
    da = rioxarray.open_rasterio(p, masked=True).squeeze()
    vmin, vmax = np.nanpercentile(da.values, [2, 98])
    da.plot(ax=ax, cmap="RdBu_r", vmin=vmin, vmax=vmax, add_colorbar=True)
    ax.set_title(f"{label.title()} anomaly — Tanacross")
    ax.set_aspect("equal")
    print(f"{label}: shape={da.shape}, p2={vmin:.1f}, p98={vmax:.1f}")
plt.tight_layout()
```

## Day 2 status

| Dataset | Records | Notes |
|---|---|---|
| MRDS | 48 total / 13 porphyry Cu-Mo | bbox query against `search-bbox.php` |
| ARDF (TC) | 50 total / 18 porphyry | full-dataset download, filtered by `quad_250` |
| Kenorland collars | 1 hole (stub) | digitize others on Day 5 |
| AGDB4 samples | 4,689 | `Geol_DeDuped.txt` clipped to AOI, to Parquet |
| Geology (SIM 3340) | 940 polygons | `AKStategeol_poly` detailed layer |
| DEM (GLO-30) | 4259 × 3930 px (30 m) | 438–1706 m elevation |
| Sentinel-2 L2A | 6 bands × 2130 × 1966 px (60 m) | 6 cleanest scenes (cloud < 0.3%), Jul–Aug 2024 |
| Magnetic | 276 × 254 px | USGS US+Canada composite, AOI-clipped |
| Gravity | 69 × 63 px | USGS US+Canada composite, AOI-clipped |

All datasets regenerable from scratch via `uv run python scripts/fetch.py`
(see [`scripts/fetch.py`](../scripts/fetch.py)).

The hero plot overlays MRDS + ARDF occurrences on the DEM with porphyry
Cu-Mo records flagged — these are the high-confidence positive-label seeds
for Day 3.

## What's next (Day 3)

- Join AGDB4 BV_*.txt tables onto the sample parquet to produce per-element
  rasters (Cu, Mo, Au, Ag, As, Sb, Pb, Zn, Bi, Te) over a 500 m–1 km analysis grid.
- Compute Sentinel-2 alteration indices (iron-oxide B4/B2, ferrous B11/B8, clay B11/B12).
- Compute DEM derivatives (slope, TRI).
- Build fault-proximity + lithology-class features from SIM 3340.
- Sample everything onto the common grid.
- Train a logistic-regression baseline with spatial CV; report PR-AUC +
  success-rate curve.

## What this doesn't prove yet

The data is here and shaped correctly; nothing more. No features, no model, no
prospectivity map, no validation. Day 3 is where the interesting work starts.
